message:
fix(classes,flow): fix node removal edge cleanup and extract flow_terminals helper

Graph node removal (fnx-classes):
- Fix remove_node to properly clean up both sides of adjacency: remove node
  from each neighbor's adjacency list before removing the node itself
- Use edges.retain() for O(E) edge cleanup instead of per-edge remove_edge
- Prevents stale adjacency entries that could cause ghost edges

Flow algorithms (fnx-python):
- Extract flow_terminals() helper that validates source/sink are different
  nodes with a clear NetworkXError when they match
- DRY refactor: maximum_flow, maximum_flow_value, minimum_cut_value,
  minimum_cut all use the shared helper

Tests:
- Add flow algorithm test cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -108,3 +108,28 @@ def test_flow_bindings_respect_edge_direction(self, fnx, nx):
         fnx_cut_val = fnx.minimum_cut_value(DG_fnx, "s", "t")
         nx_cut_val = nx.minimum_cut_value(DG_nx, "s", "t")
         assert abs(fnx_cut_val - nx_cut_val) < 1e-9
+
+    def test_flow_bindings_reject_same_source_and_sink(self, fnx, nx):
+        DG_fnx, DG_nx = _directed_flow_pair(
+            fnx,
+            nx,
+            [
+                ("s", "a", 3.0),
+                ("a", "t", 2.0),
+            ],
+        )
+
+        for name in (
+            "maximum_flow",
+            "maximum_flow_value",
+            "minimum_cut",
+            "minimum_cut_value",
+        ):
+            fnx_flow_fn = getattr(fnx, name)
+            nx_flow_fn = getattr(nx, name)
+
+            with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match="source and sink are the same node"):
+                fnx_flow_fn(DG_fnx, "s", "s")
+
+            with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match="source and sink are the same node"):
+                nx_flow_fn(DG_nx, "s", "s")
